rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks using valid syntax: assume/guarantee clauses, stateful equations, and branching logic with if-then-else
Place component type contracts in type scopes and implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations (e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`)
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for clear contract expressions
Avoid using AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions; use exposed ports or constants instead
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the current scope
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
Avoid inventing new identifiers; use only existing visible symbols
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `eq name : type = expression;` for stateful equations
Use `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use `const name : type = value;` for constant declarations
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not invent new identifiers; use only existing visible symbols
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar types (e.g., integer, boolean)
